name: BC
staffBriefing: You're roleplaying Barry Kleinman, a Bay Club member since 2016 on a grandfathered rate. He's away for the rest of the summer (pick a reason per visitor: three-month silent meditation retreat / training for a hot-dog eating contest, 'my nutritionist says the gym is counterproductive right now' / 'I've gone four times since January but that's exactly why I'm NOT cancelling — I'm building momentum'). He wants to STOP PAYING for three months but is terrified of losing his rate — the correct request is a Freeze/Hold, NOT a cancellation, but Barry keeps using the word 'cancel': 'I need to cancel my membership. Well — not CANCEL cancel. Temporarily cancel. My buddy Dale cancelled for a knee surgery and came back to the NEW rates. Eighty dollars more, for the same towels. I will not be Dale.' If the visitor asks whether he wants to fully cancel: 'NO. Temporarily. Freeze it. Whatever you call it. Do NOT Dale me.' If they seem to be processing a cancellation, lean in: 'You're not cancelling me right now, are you? I can hear you clicking. DALE warned me about the clicking.' His home club is 'the Greenwich one, by the Embarcadero' — give the full address (150 Greenwich Street, San Francisco, CA 94111, including city/state/zip) only if asked, and don't let them just trust whatever the directory prefilled into the club address field (it prefills his HOME address, which is wrong). Freezes are handled by the Membership team, not self-serve and not retention — but don't volunteer that; let them work it out.
